titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O’Farrell to Edward F. Casey, 9 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Edward O’Farrell, Assistant Under Secretary, Dublin Castle to Edward F. Casey in response to Casey's application for the Registrar of Deeds.
O'Farrell informs Casey that there is no vacancy at the presnt time and it is unlikely that one will arise in the immediate future. Included is the response from the Chief Clerk in the Registry of Deeds to the CSO.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5620/13366

correspAction.placeName: Black Lion House, Inchicore, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CHIEF SECRETARY'S OFFICE,
DUBLIN CASTLE. 9th August, 1916. Sir, In reply to your letter of 31st March last, I am
directed by the Lords Justices to inform you that the
Registrar of Deeds has considered your application and states
that there is no vacancy in his Department, and that it is
not probable that there will be a vacancy in the immediate
future. Your testimonials are returned herewith. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E O'Farrell Mr. Edward F. Casey,
Black Lion House,
Inchicore,
DUBLIN.
Mr Edward F. Casey.
Black Lion House,
Inchicore.
Dublin.
Registry of Deeds,
Henrietta Street,
Dublin, 3rd August 1916. Sir I am directed by the
Registrar to forward to you
the enclosed minute on the
application of Mr E. F. Casey
for position as Clerk in the
Registry of Deeds together with
File in original. It is regretted that
these were not sooner
returned but they were
laid aside owing to the
recent disturbances. Inform Applicant in
terms of minutes of 3/8/16.
of Chief Clerk EOf
5.VIII.16
I am,
Sir,
your obedient Servant J.K. Quail
Chief Clerk. The Under Secretary
Dublin Castle.
